Title: Bikaverin as a molecular weapon: enhancing Fusarium oxysporum pathogenicity in bananas via rhizosphere microbiome manipulation

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. cubense Tropical Race 4
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. cubense Tropical Race 4
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced under acidic pH 4, yeast extract/organic nitrogen, and during co-culture competition with Bacillus velezensis, Botrytis cinerea, and Actinomycetes strain DMS16689; low at pH 7 or 9 and with NaNO3
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced under acidic pH 4, yeast extract/organic nitrogen, and during co-culture competition with Bacillus velezensis, Botrytis cinerea, and Actinomycetes strain DMS16689; low at pH 7 or 9 and with NaNO3
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Targeting FocBik1 or bikaverin biosynthesis could reduce Foc TR4 ecological fitness and indirect pathogenicity by preventing rhizosphere microbiome manipulation; useful disease-management target rather than host nutrient-improvement target
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Acidic pH; nitrogen source/type and scarcity; microbial competition/co-culture with Bacillus velezensis, Botrytis cinerea, and Actinomycetes strain DMS16689; sterile versus nonsterile rhizosphere context
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the initial formation of prebikaverin from pathway precursors in bikaverin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for bikaverin production; deletion abolishes bikaverin accumulation, reduces suppression of beneficial rhizosphere bacteria such as Bacillus, lowers Fusarium enrichment in the rhizosphere, and reduces pathogenicity in banana under nonsterile conditions, but not under sterile or autoclaved conditions
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial secondary metabolite that suppresses certain soil bacteria and fungi and indirectly enhances banana wilt by reshaping the rhizosphere microbiome
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FocBik1 encodes the key type I PKS required for bikaverin biosynthesis in Foc TR4. 2. Knockout of FocBik1 abolished detectable bikaverin production and red pigmentation. 3. Loss of FocBik1 did not block root infection under sterile conditions, indicating bikaverin is not a direct virulence factor in host tissue invasion. 4. FocBik1-dependent bikaverin suppressed Bacillus and altered rhizosphere bacterial/fungal community structure, thereby indirectly enhancing pathogenicity in banana. 5. FocBik1 transcription was strongly induced by acidic pH, organic nitrogen/YE, and microbial competition.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Deletion had little effect on vegetative growth, slightly reduced conidiation, and caused slightly increased sensitivity to sorbitol, H2O2, and Congo red. Bikaverin showed no evident self-toxicity to Foc TR4 under the tested conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion slightly reduces conidiation and slightly increases sensitivity to osmotic, oxidative, and cell-wall stress; no major vegetative-growth defect reported
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 16S rRNA amplicon sequencing and ITS amplicon sequencing, NCBI BioProject PRJNA1197142
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms pathway intermediate leading to target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene knockout; complementation; LC-MS metabolite profiling; RT-qPCR; pathogenicity assays; rhizosphere microbiome amplicon sequencing; antimicrobial bioassays
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fungal culture broth and mycelial/intracellular extracts
